title: What's Under the Platform? — Cross-Section Reveal for Push'em All
hook_3s: Camera slices through the bright blue water beside the platform — revealing hungry sharks circling directly below the red stickman crowd about to be pushed in.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Top-down hero shot of the sandy platform. Camera tilts and the world splits in half like a diorama — water surface peels away to expose sharks swimming under the arena. Overlay: 'What's down there?'
3-7s: Snap back to gameplay POV. Player swipes, the stick expands, and a red stickman crowd is shoved toward the edge. Combo multiplier ticks up: x2, x3, x4.
7-11s: Cross-section view returns mid-push — we follow a red stickman tumbling off the platform and SPLASH into the shark zone below. Coin counter spikes, 'SIZE UP!' power-up triggers.
11-14s: Wider stick sweeps a massive crowd off in one satisfying arc, level progress bar fills to FINISH.
14-15s: End card with logo, tap-to-play pulse, CTA.
visual_direction: Lean fully into the Push'em All DNA: minimalist 3D, sandy/green platform (#56a38b), vibrant blue water (#2a7dfa), bright red stickman enemies (#e3001b). The 'cross-section' twist is achieved by literally cutting away the water surface to expose a stylized underwater layer — same flat-shaded minimalist 3D, no realism. Keep the energetic/competitive HUD (progress timeline top, combo multiplier center, coin counter top-right). Camera is dynamic: orbiting reveal into top-down gameplay, then a clean diorama split for the payoff. No muted slate/khaki tones from the source archetype — translate the curiosity beat into our bright primary palette.
rationale: Voodoo's current rotation on Push'em All is all straight gameplay videos and a playable — zero curiosity-driven 'reveal' hooks despite curiosity·animation trending across 3 advertisers in the scan. This brief brings the underrepresented cross-section/reveal angle but restages it safely as 'what lurks beneath the arena' so the 3-second pivot to core swipe-push gameplay is honest and IPM-safe. UA team: test against the existing pure-gameplay videos as the curiosity-hook variant; expect lift on CTR with comparable install intent.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, Push'em All in-game moment: minimalist 3D sandy-green platform (#56a38b) floating in vibrant blue water (#2a7dfa), camera angled to reveal a stylized cross-section cutaway of the water beside the platform exposing flat-shaded cartoon sharks swimming underneath. On the platform surface: a dense crowd of bright red stickman figures (#e3001b) clustered near the edge, an expanding push-stick mechanic visible. Authentic game HUD: level progress timeline along the top, coin counter top-right, combo multiplier center. On-screen text overlay 'What's under the platform?' in bold white sans-serif. Bright, contrasting colors, simple stickman-like figures, minimalist 3D visual style, energetic competitive mood, no realism, no muted tones.

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Push'em All gameplay: top-down-ish 3D view of the sandy-green platform (#56a38b) surrounded by vibrant blue water (#2a7dfa), an expanding push-stick sweeping a crowd of bright red stickman enemies (#e3001b) off the edge, several red stickmen mid-air tumbling toward the water. HUD shows progress timeline, coin counter, and a large 'COMBO x4!' overlay in bold yellow. Minimalist 3D with bright contrasting colors and simple stickman-like figures, energetic competitive UI, authentic Push'em All chrome.

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Push'em All cross-section payoff shot: split diorama view showing the sandy-green platform (#56a38b) on top and the underwater layer in vibrant blue (#2a7dfa) below, a single red stickman figure (#e3001b) caught mid-splash falling from platform into water where stylized flat-shaded sharks circle. 'SIZE UP!' power-up notification overlay in bright accent red, coin counter visible top-right, level progress timeline along top edge. Minimalist 3D, bright contrasting colors, simple stickman-like figures, no realism, authentic in-game Push'em All look.
